behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm’s own quality bound (local computation), not OS/network/LLM behavior. MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets algorithmic correctness, not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real infra is required; deterministic fixtures with known optimal baselines can expose regressions. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified because the behavior text is only about heuristic-vs-optimal route length ratio.
behaviors.test_description: Run deterministic algorithm tests on representative stop sets (including edge distributions), compute heuristic length vs precomputed optimal length, and assert ratio <= 1.5 for each case up to 20 stops.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This failure commonly appears at module boundaries: distance output, road-type speed config lookup, and estimator wiring (e.g., unit mismatch or wrong config key). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess need, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text limits scope to deterministic computation from distance + config.
behaviors.test_description: Wire route-distance provider + speed-config resolver + time-window estimator in-process; feed multi-road-type routes and assert computed segment and total windows match expected values, including unit-conversion edge cases.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is a pure validation rule and arithmetic output (reject + excess). MOCK-MISS: no required multi-module interaction in the behavior statement. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: none apply. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified because text specifies deterministic comparison against remaining shift hours and excess calculation.
behaviors.test_description: Test validator function with boundary inputs (equal, just under, just over, large overrun) and assert acceptance/rejection plus exact excess amount.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is orchestration across modules: geocoding result handling, filtering, optimizer input, and skipped-address reporting. Integration brake: (a) real network not required because behavior is specifically 'API returns no results' (a response-shape case reproducible with stub), (b) no AI dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior does not require timeout/transport/real HTTP conditions, only correct handling of a no-results payload.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process service wiring with a stub geocoder that returns no results for one address; assert that address is omitted from optimization, remaining route is produced, and skipped-address report contains exactly the failed address.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Failure is often at handoff between dedup/aggregation logic and route construction (ensuring one consolidated stop is actually visited once). MOCK-MISS: unit-only tests of dedupe function can miss downstream double-visit regressions. Integration brake: (a) no OS/network/subprocess need, (b) no AI dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior is deterministic local data processing and routing wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process pipeline from input normalization through route generation; provide duplicated addresses with distinct items and assert one stop in final route, merged items preserved, and no second visit instance.
